text:
Imane Khelif’s father says his daughter was ‘raised as a girl’ and has always been proficient in various sports during her childhood.

Khelif guaranteed the first Olympic medal of her career on Saturdayfollowing a win over Hungary’s Anna Luca Hamori in the women’s 66kg quarter final.

Khelif, who won all three rounds in an impressive performance against Hamori, was overcome with emotion after her victory and left the ring in tears of joy alongside her two coaches.

Khelif’s participation inParishas beenmet with controversyafter she was disqualified from the Women’s World Championships last year for failing International Boxing Association (IBA)gendereligibility rules.

Both Khelif andTaiwan’s Lin Yu-ting, who also failed a gender eligibility test with the IBA, competed at the Olympics in Tokyo three years ago but did not win a medal.

Khelif, 25, has always competed in the women’s boxing division and has identified as female since birth.

And in an interview with French broadcaster BFM TV, her father, Omar Khelif, said: ‘My child is a girl. She was raised as a girl.

‘She is a strong girl. I raised her to work and be brave.’

During the interview, Omar Khelif also produced an official Algerian registration document which reveals his daughter’s date of birth on May 2, 1999 and the fact that she is registered as a female.

Omar Khelif added that his daughter ‘has a strong will at work and in training’.

He also revealed: ‘Since she was little, her passion has been sport. In all other sports, she was always in the lead, in running and football.’

Khelif’s coach, Mohamed Chaoua, has also hit out at the criticism the Algerian boxer has received.

‘It is hard, she has suffered a lot, as a child and now as a champion, she has suffered so much during these Games,’ Chaoua said before Khelif’s win on Saturday.

‘Where is the humanity? Where are the associations for women’s rights? She is a victim.’

Khelif will now face Thailand’s Janjaem Suwannapheng in the semi final on August 6 at 21.34pm, but is guaranteed at least a bronze medal regardless of the result of that fight.

title: Imane Khelif's father responds to Olympics boxing controversy | Metro News
description: Imane Khelif's father says his daughter was 'raised as a girl' and has always been proficient in various sports during her childhood.
